unresolved: Field-vs-parameter ambiguity: '(a field added to the query request path)' reads like a struct field added to backend.QueryDataRequest, which would NOT change any method signature — then only sites that read/populate the field must change. If instead the method signature gains a parameter, then EVERY caller (including all test invocations) must change. The two readings give very different scopes; I scoped to all handler implementations + request-constructing/forwarding call sites.

Hundreds of test-file invocation call sites pass an already-built request (e.g. pkg/tsdb/{mssql,mysql,grafana-postgresql,cloudwatch,azuremonitor,graphite,opentsdb}/*_test.go and the clientmiddleware *_test.go suites). These change only under the parameter-addition reading; I did not enumerate each enclosing table-test closure.

gRPC/proto boundary: pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/client_proto.go:protoClient.QueryData operates on pluginv2.QueryDataRequest (generated proto, defined in the SDK). If the new field must cross the wire, the proto + conversion in ClientV2.QueryData must change, but the proto definition itself is out of this repo.

Interface C path (data.QueryDataRequest): qsDsClient.QueryData calls in pkg/services/query/query.go:handleQuerySingleDatasource and pkg/expr/nodes.go (executeDSNodesGrouped, DSNode.Execute) go through expr.ConvertBackendRequestToDataRequest; if the new field must propagate to the k8s flow, that converter and data.QueryDataRequest also need the field. Included the enclosing functions but not the converter.

Excluded interface B (pkg/services/query/query.go:ServiceImpl.QueryData and its callers in pkg/api/ds_query.go, pkg/services/publicdashboards/service/query.go) as the high-level HTTP query service, not the datasource QueryData handler — flagging in case the intended 'request path' starts there.

Mux indirection: testdata and azuremonitor dispatch via SDK datasource.QueryTypeMux (s.queryMux.QueryData) to per-query-type handler funcs (e.g. executeTimeSeriesQuery) that are not named QueryData; not counted as handler impls but they are in the request path.

unresolved: Scope boundary of 'a field added to the request path': if the change is a NEW PARAMETER on QueryData (signature change), all listed call sites must change. If instead it is only a new struct field on backend.QueryDataRequest (no signature change), then NO call site needs editing and only producers/forwarders that should populate the field do — the two readings yield very different site sets. I assumed the signature-change reading per the issue wording.

Internal cloudwatch helpers that take *backend.QueryDataRequest but are NOT the handler (e.g. log_sync_query.go:executeSyncLogQuery, log_anomalies_query.go:executeLogAnomaliesQuery, and per-action execute* funcs) were EXCLUDED as not part of the handler signature; if the added field must be threaded through them they would also change.

Excluded as different methods/signatures (verified): the DTO-based query service (pkg/services/query ServiceImpl.QueryData/queryData/QueryDataNew/executeConcurrentQueries, pkg/api/ds_query.go, publicdashboards QueryDataService.QueryData, registry/apis/query/query.go:QueryData), the data.QueryDataRequest client path (pkg/registry/apis/query/client/plugin.go:pluginClient.QueryData signature, qsDsClient.QueryData in query.go/nodes.go), and the gRPC proto path (grpcplugin client_proto.go / client_v2 DataClient.QueryData). If the field also propagates into data.QueryDataRequest or the protobuf, those would expand the set.

Test call sites in huge table-driven files were collapsed to their single enclosing Test function (e.g. TestMSSQL, TestIntegrationPostgres/MySQL); each such function contains many individual call lines. I did not separately verify every subtest closure, only the enclosing function symbol.

Dispatch through SDK-owned types (backend.HandlerMiddleware chains and datasource.QueryTypeMux) is via interfaces in the plugin-sdk-go dependency (not in this repo); I enumerated every concrete in-repo implementation/closure by grep, but cannot see SDK-side call sites.

unresolved: backend.QueryDataHandlerFunc-typed handlers share the same func signature via the type alias: testdata scenario handlers (pkg/tsdb/grafana-testdata-datasource/scenarios.go , csv_data.go handleCsvContentScenario/handleCsvFileScenario, usa_stats.go handleUSAScenario, instrumentScenarioHandler) and anonymous mux closures (azuremonitor newQueryMux). These must change only if the change is a parameter/signature change to the func type, not if it is merely a new field on the QueryDataRequest struct (pointer type unchanged); the issue text is ambiguous on this.

Numerous *_test.go invocation call sites also reference the handler and would need mechanical updates if the parameter list changes (e.g. pkg/tsdb/mssql/mssql_test.go, pkg/tsdb/mysql/mysql_test.go, pkg/tsdb/grafana-postgresql-datasource/postgres_test.go, pkg/tsdb/cloudwatch/*_test.go, pkg/tsdb/graphite/query_test.go, pkg/tsdb/opentsdb/opentsdb_test.go, pkg/tsdb/azuremonitor/azuremonitor_test.go, pkg/tsdb/grafana-pyroscope-datasource/instance_test.go, pkg/tsdb/parca/plugin_test.go, pkg/tsdb/grafana-testdata-datasource/sims/flight_path_test.go, all clientmiddleware *_test.go MiddlewareHandler.QueryData calls, pkg/plugins/manager/client/client_test.go TestQueryData, pkg/services/pluginsintegration/plugintest/plugins_test.go). These individual test functions were not enumerated due to volume.

Internal helper functions that receive *backend.QueryDataRequest but are not the handler nor direct callers (e.g. cloudwatch executeLogActions/executeTimeSeriesQuery, zipkin/jaeger queryData, influxql.Query) would only change if the new field must be threaded through them; out of scope for a pure handler-signature change.

Ambiguity in issue wording: if a field is merely ADDED to the backend.QueryDataRequest struct (same *backend.QueryDataRequest type), no implementation or call-site breaks at compile time and only request constructors/consumers need updating; if it is a true method-SIGNATURE change (new param), every implementation and every call site (including all tests) must change. I enumerated for the signature-change interpretation but did not individually list every test call site.

Numerous test CALL-SITE functions invoke the handler and would need updating under a signature change but are NOT individually enumerated: e.g. tsdb/cloudwatch (log_actions_test, time_series_query_test, log_sync_query_test, log_anomalies_query_test, annotation_query_test — 150+ calls), tsdb/mssql/mssql_test.go, tsdb/mysql/mysql_test.go & mysql_snapshot_test.go, tsdb/graphite/query_test.go, tsdb/azuremonitor/azuremonitor_test.go, tsdb/opentsdb/opentsdb_test.go, tsdb/grafana-testdata-datasource/sims/flight_path_test.go (SimulationEngine), all clientmiddleware/*_test.go Test* funcs, pkg/plugins/manager/client/client_test.go:TestQueryData, pkg/services/pluginsintegration/plugintest/plugins_test.go:verifyPluginQuery, pkg/services/query/query_test.go.

unresolved: Interpretation of 'a field is added to the query request path': I treated it as a change to the handler method signature (backend.QueryDataHandler.QueryData), so all implementations + callers + QueryDataHandlerFunc closure literals must change. If instead only a field is ADDED to the backend.QueryDataRequest struct (no signature change), then NONE of these sites need editing — the set would be near-empty. This single ambiguity is why complete=false.

Excluded as a DIFFERENT same-named method (higher-level query service, signature QueryData(ctx, user, skipDSCache, reqDTO)): pkg/services/query/query.go ServiceImpl.QueryData / executeConcurrentQueries / package-level QueryData, pkg/services/query/query_service_mock.go FakeQueryService.QueryData, pkg/api/ds_query.go HTTPServer.QueryMetricsV2, pkg/services/publicdashboards/service/query.go GetQueryDataResponse, pkg/registry/apis/query/query.go handlePreparedQuery, and pkg/services/query/query_test.go TestIntegrationQueryDataMultipleSources/TestIntegrationQueryDataWithQSDSClient.

Note pkg/registry/apis/query/client/plugin.go pluginClient.QueryData IS included, but only because its body calls the backend handler (d.pluginClient.QueryData) — if the field is threaded through data.QueryDataRequest as well, this and the qsDsClient/k8sReq call sites in pkg/expr/nodes.go and pkg/services/query/query.go could also need edits.
Excluded as generated gRPC proto (pluginv2.DataClient.QueryData, distinct signature): pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/client_proto.go protoClient.QueryData.
